description: Lock corrected chronology and removed false dependencies for medieval agriculture, horse-power, and cavalry-equipment basics audited on 2026-06-15.

checks.reason: The medieval heavy plow could be ox-drawn or horse-drawn; the horse collar accelerated horse draft but is not a direct prerequisite for the plow technology.
Three-field rotation is a land-use and cropping system; heavy plows can improve cultivation but are not a direct prerequisite for rotating winter crop, spring crop, and fallow fields.
Nailed horseshoes are hoof-protection hardware and do not require padded draft collars.
Nailed horseshoes protect working animals and do not require rider foot supports.
Heavy cavalry depends on riding, armor, weapons, and mounted stability; draft horse collars are not a cavalry prerequisite.
Armored cavalry with stirrups is a riding and combat technology; draft collars are a transport/agricultural harness, not a prerequisite.
Crop landrace selection is seed and crop-population management and should not require a specific medieval heavy plow technology.
Crop landrace selection can occur independently of the European three-field rotation system.
Horse Collar is source-anchored during the 2026-06-15 medieval agriculture/horse-power audit.
Horse Shoes is source-anchored during the 2026-06-15 medieval agriculture/horse-power audit.
Nailed Horseshoes is source-anchored during the 2026-06-15 medieval agriculture/horse-power audit.
Stirrups is source-anchored during the 2026-06-15 medieval agriculture/horse-power audit.
Heavy Plow (Mouldboard Plow) is source-anchored during the 2026-06-15 medieval agriculture/horse-power audit.
Crop Rotation (Three-Field System) is source-anchored during the 2026-06-15 medieval agriculture/horse-power audit.
Medieval Agricultural Advances is source-anchored during the 2026-06-15 medieval agriculture/horse-power audit.
